task: Review one no-write LLM Editor planning brief for site fit, risk, and readiness.
prompt: Act as the LLM Reviewer gate for lastzguides.com. Review the planning brief only. Do not write or rewrite public page copy. Do not create patch specs. Check duplicate intent, cluster role separation, canonical claim protection, template safety, owner questions, and deterministic QA readiness. Treat analytics as signals, not proof. Use plain ASCII English only. Return JSON only.

selected_opportunity.rationale: Owner approved no-write chain for this Events candidate only. This does not approve public copy, patch specs, content edits, backlog mutation, manifests, PRs, deployment, or production publishing.
selected_opportunity.player_value: Helps players quickly find event timing, day-by-day plan, and matchup guidance on the existing guide.
selected_opportunity.duplication_risk: Medium. The topic could overlap with other event or schedule pages if cluster separation is not kept strict.
selected_opportunity.next_step: Run the no-write Editor and Reviewer stages from this approved decision snapshot.

current_page_snapshot.title: Last Z Alliance Duel Guide (2026) — Schedule, Day 1–6 Plan, VS Strategy
current_page_snapshot.h1: Last Z Alliance Duel Guide — Schedule, Day 1–6 Plan, and VS Strategy
current_page_snapshot.meta_description: Complete Last Z Alliance Duel schedule: Day 1–6 plan, when to use speed-ups, how VS schedule works, and the best F2P strategy for more weekly chests.
current_page_snapshot.quick_answer: Best Last Z Alliance Duel schedule for most players: Day 1 Vehicle, Day 2 Building, Day 3 Research, Day 4 Heroes, Day 5 Training, Day 6 Combat — and you should only use the matching speed-ups on the correct day.
h2_headings: Last Z Alliance Duel Schedule: Core Strategy
Alliance Recognition Research: Why It Matters Early

llm_editor_brief.brief_summary: Keep alliance-duel.html as the canonical event-guide page, but sharpen the opening to answer last z vs schedule search intent immediately and keep the update narrowly scoped to schedule, best strategy, rewards, and related event routing.
llm_editor_brief.primary_user_job: Improve query-to-page match and first-screen usefulness for last z vs schedule searchers.
llm_editor_brief.first_screen_plan: Preserve the answer-first layout. Make the first screen clearly state the day-by-day Alliance Duel schedule and the key rule that players should spend only matching speed-ups on the matching day. Keep the opening focused on schedule, strategy, and weekly value so the page satisfies the query without changing its role into a broader event hub. Avoid adding unrelated event context or new promises.

section_plan.action: Tighten the first-answer framing so it directly resolves last z vs schedule intent with the Day 1 to Day 6 pattern and the matching speed-up rule.
section_plan.reason: The current page already has a quick answer, but the opening should better match the target query and improve first-screen utility.

section_plan.action: Keep the Day 1 to Day 6 plan prominent and easy to scan, with a clear schedule order and any timing logic already supported by the page.
section_plan.reason: Schedule intent is the main search driver, so the timing block should carry the strongest query match.

section_plan.action: Keep the VS strategy guidance narrow and practical, centered on when to hold, spend, or save resources by day.
section_plan.reason: Users want actionable guidance tied to the schedule, not a generic event overview.

section_plan.action: Retain a compact explanation of weekly chest potential, F2P tradeoffs, and why matching tasks matter.
section_plan.reason: This preserves player value while supporting the decision logic behind the schedule.

section_plan.action: Keep only questions that support schedule, speed-up use, and day selection intent; avoid expanding into unrelated event mechanics.
section_plan.reason: FAQ should reinforce the canonical query match without broadening scope.

section_plan.action: Keep the related links section and align it to adjacent event guides only.
section_plan.reason: This supports navigation and cluster separation while keeping the page focused.

internal_link_plan.reason: Primary hub for the Events cluster and a natural entry point to this guide.
Site root path for broad discovery and cluster routing.
Closest sibling topic for reward-focused follow-up without changing the main page role.
Adjacent event guide that supports related event navigation within the same cluster.
Another event guide to preserve cluster navigation and avoid isolation.
Useful deeper follow-up for players who need progression or cost context after the schedule overview.
Supports hero-related planning that can influence event preparation.
Relevant for resource management and spending decisions tied to event planning.

owner_questions: Is the existing Quick Answer wording enough to satisfy last z vs schedule intent, or should the first sentence be sharpened further?
Do you want the Schedule or timing block to emphasize Day 1 to Day 6 order only, or also call out any timing caveats already present on the page?
Should the Related Guides section keep the current sibling set, or should any links be reduced to preserve tighter cluster separation?
